candidate_problems.text: How can 'real domain invariants' be defined and distinguished from corpus-specific reusable structure in a non-circular, operational way? The conjecture hinges on a boundary between ontology-level hidden causes and merely stable artifacts of the training distribution, but it does not specify a criterion for telling them apart from behavior alone.

What are the necessary and sufficient conditions on the data-generating process, model class, and optimization dynamics under which latent-state representations are actually the minimum-description-length solution to next-token prediction? The conjecture appeals to compression pressure, but leaves open whether practical learners truly converge to invariant-tracking latents rather than alternative compressed shortcuts.

How much and what kind of transformation diversity is required before shortcut statistics become more expensive than representing a shared hidden cause? The conjecture says paraphrastic and contextual variation can force invariant learning, but the threshold, geometry, and measurable properties of that variation remain unspecified.
